expert_rss.title: War Department Signs $9.7B Technology Deal With Dell for Microsoft Services
expert_rss.summary: The War Department signed a $9.7 billion, five-year enterprisewide contract to purchase Microsoft Services, a move expected to save the department an initial $422 million annually.

expert_rss.title: Personal Property Activity Expands PCS Support Across Joint Force
expert_rss.summary: Now that peak permanent change of station season is underway, the Personal Property Activity is providing tips and operating a 24/7 call center until Sept. 15 to ensure support is available for service members and their families preparing for a PCS.

expert_rss.title: Moody Air Force Base Installs Refueling Probe, Improves Efficiency
expert_rss.summary: Airmen assigned to Moody Air Force Base, Ga., installed the base's first air refueling probe on an A-10C Thunderbolt II subsonic attack aircraft, enhancing its ability to support combat search and rescue and close-air support operations.

expert_rss.title: Hegseth Praises U.S. Military Efforts to Protect Nigerian Christians From ISIS
expert_rss.summary: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth praised recent efforts the U.S. military has made to counter the Islamic State and its ongoing murder of Christians in Nigeria.

expert_rss.title: Sword 26: Cyber Defenders Train in Estonia, Secure Critical Infrastructure
expert_rss.summary: U.S. soldiers and National Guard cyber professionals trained with members of the Estonian Defense Forces to defend the networks that modern societies rely on daily during a Sword 26 simulated exercise.

expert_rss.title: MQ-28 Ghost Bat Now Flying Over The Pacific From U.S. Navy Base
expert_rss.summary:
Boeing says tests off California are about showing the Australian-born MQ-28's maturity and promoting sales, which could include ones to the Pentagon.
The post MQ-28 Ghost Bat Now Flying Over The Pacific From U.S. Navy Base appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: New Counter-Drone Optimized Pantsir Air Defense System Being Deployed Atop Skyscrapers In Moscow
expert_rss.summary:
A video showing a Pantsir-SMD-E being airlifted onto a skyscraper underscores Russian attempts to shield Moscow from increasing Ukrainian drone attacks.
The post New Counter-Drone Optimized Pantsir Air Defense System Being Deployed Atop Skyscrapers In Moscow appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Army Wants More Sensor-Laden Surveillance Balloons Over The Pacific
expert_rss.summary:
High-altitude balloons are an increasingly critical part of the Army's future combat strategy, from providing networking to sensing to delivering kinetic effects.
The post Army Wants More Sensor-Laden Surveillance Balloons Over The Pacific appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Severity Of America’s Depleted Advanced Weapons Stockpiles Detailed In New Report
expert_rss.summary:
America's stockpile of critical standoff and air and missile defense weapons is now a strategic vulnerability foes could take advantage of.
The post Severity Of America’s Depleted Advanced Weapons Stockpiles Detailed In New Report appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Ukrainian Gripen Fighters To Arrive In 2027, Long-Range Meteor Missiles Claimed To Be Included
expert_rss.summary:
Sweden’s transfer of Gripens will give Ukraine more than a new fighter, pairing its trademark dispersed operations with Meteor missiles and radar planes.
The post Ukrainian Gripen Fighters To Arrive In 2027, Long-Range Meteor Missiles Claimed To Be Included appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Search For New 5.56mm “Flyweight” Special Operations Machine Gun To Kick Off Soon
expert_rss.summary:
The Flyweight Assault Machine Gun will provide a replacement for a lightweight special operations-specific version of the M249 SAW.
The post Search For New 5.56mm “Flyweight” Special Operations Machine Gun To Kick Off Soon appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.summary:
Ukraine has a voracious demand for ground drones as they increasingly perform logistics, rescue and combat operations.
The post Inside The Effort To Build Ukraine’s Ground Robot Arsenal appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Airbus Looks To Sweden’s Saab As Europe’s Sixth-Gen Fighter Plans Unravel
expert_rss.summary:
As FCAS tensions deepen, Airbus is exploring alternatives with Saab, raising the prospect of a shakeup in Europe’s future air combat plans.
The post Airbus Looks To Sweden’s Saab As Europe’s Sixth-Gen Fighter Plans Unravel appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Congress Moves To Block Trump Class Battleship Work Until Its Key Weapons Are Mature
expert_rss.summary:
Such a delay could end the battleship program before it really starts, and the House Armed Services Committee is also demanding answers on the new frigate.
The post Congress Moves To Block Trump Class Battleship Work Until Its Key Weapons Are Mature appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: F/A-18 Super Hornets Eyed To Replace Navy’s Remaining F-5 Adversaries
expert_rss.summary:
Swapping dated F-5s for F/A-18E/Fs would give the Navy a more threat representative adversary fleet to emulate advanced enemies, like China.
The post F/A-18 Super Hornets Eyed To Replace Navy’s Remaining F-5 Adversaries appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: White House proposes all federal employees sign nondisclosure agreements
expert_rss.summary: Following a recent string of unathorized disclosures, or leaks, the Trump administration announced their intent to draft NDAs for federal workers.

expert_rss.title: US Army turns resupply drone into rocket launcher in new test
expert_rss.summary: Though the drone was designed to haul supplies across the battlefield, the Army recently tried turning it into a rocket launcher.

expert_rss.title: Double amputee paratrooper to jump into Normandy for 82nd anniversary of D-Day
expert_rss.summary: Fourteen years after losing both his legs to an IED, Jon Harmon will step out of a C-47 and jump into same spot the 82nd Airborne fought on D‑Day.

expert_rss.title: 22nd MEU, Iwo Jima ARG head home after 10-month deployment
expert_rss.summary: The Iwo Jima ARG made headlines earlier this year when it took part in the Jan. 3 capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro.

expert_rss.title: US troops are reportedly being targeted using location data, Pentagon says
expert_rss.summary: U.S. forces deployed to war zones have been targeted using commercially available location data, according to reports fielded by military officials.

expert_rss.title: US carries out new strikes in Iran against military site, official says
expert_rss.summary: The U.S. military carried out new strikes overnight in Iran targeting a military site that officials believed posed a threat to U.S. forces.

expert_rss.title: Pentagon eyes drone testing ground in Mississippi
expert_rss.summary: U.S. Special Operations Command is looking to build a new testing ground for drones in Mississippi.

wikipedia.notes: At 8:55 p.m. local time, a suicide bomber and multiple fighters detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near a security post, causing the post to collapse, destroying nearby vehicles and severely damaging nearby civilian areas.
Shortly after 8:00 a.m. PKT, an explosives-laden vehicle struck one of the carriages of a shuttle train near Chaman Phatak as the train was travelling through Quetta.
